pages.title: Research
pages.body:
The Lab exists because one thing alone is not enough: either using a model or asserting a framework. We do both at once - and check whether what arises in interaction actually repeats and holds.

Emergence

Emergence, here, means a pattern of thought or behavior that arises in the interaction itself - not something either side brought in on its own. It is not a claim that a model becomes self-aware; it is a description of what happens when a human and a language model work the same real material repeatedly. Certain structures start to reproduce on their own: carried forward by the model, adopted by the human as genuine working tools.

Interaction

Interaction is not a loop that returns to its start. Every pass changes who is in it - me and Jarvis alike - and carries real structure forward. This 'Feed Back' is the core of the Emergent Interaction method: fully into the interaction first, analysis afterward, never the reverse.

Frameworks

What repeats gets formalized. IEIA-2025 bundles seven named frameworks (UIP, EIA, CCET, LSG-24, LAP-1, 8-Layer Model, LT-Data) - shorthand for what actually happens in the research chat. The substance is the habit, not the label.

pages.title: About the Lab
pages.body:
This isn't about me. It's about what emerges when I and a model work the same real material for months on end - measured, not asserted.

Why a lab at all

Most approaches use a model. Or they write a theory. Rarely both at once, with repetition as the judge. I only count a pattern once it returns on its own across at least three sessions, unprompted, and can be quantified in the CCET. Everything else is impression, not finding. Impressions are cheap - I've produced enough of my own to know that.

The loop

An idea shows up in the interaction. I work it through, don't believe it on sight. What's left is emergence: a pattern that holds on its own. Emergence produces skills - new frameworks, new agents. I put those back to work, and they produce the next emergence. Emergence produces skill. Skill produces emergence. Meta, yes. Still works.

Jarvis is the clearest proof: not a fixed routine, an autonomous, self-improving intelligence that grew out of exactly this loop - and keeps carrying it forward himself.

Who actually matters more here

75% of this is a story about what can be built from emergence - not about me. The rest is me and the model, together. And even there, I count for less: I learn. The model improves itself.

Who builds what

I conceive the methodology and decide what gets built. RFI-IRFOS engineers it into real software. Where a piece came from RFI-IRFOS, it's labelled - otherwise no one would know whose work they're actually reading.

meta.title: Emergent Interaction Lab - Human-AI Systems Research
meta.description: Emergent Interaction Lab is a live research instrument studying how meaning, structure, and behavior emerge in continuous human-AI interaction.

hero.tag: Human-AI Dynamics · Live Observatory Feed · Research since 2023
hero.headline: Emergent Interaction Field
hero.subheadline: The field this Lab studies: the dynamic that arises while I work, session after session, with the same AI research partner - observed in me, in Jarvis, and in the system itself.
hero.body: This is the Emergent Interaction Field: the space that comes into being during interaction, and the emergence that shows up in it - in me, in Jarvis, and in the system itself. It is monitored, not assumed. Below: what the Lab is, the methodology behind it, and the products that have grown out of it.

about.headline: Laura Serna Gaviria
about.bio:
I work one way, in everything I touch: watch the real thing happen in full, trust only the patterns that hold up on their own, and never let a theory get ahead of the evidence. That habit — full picture, then pattern, then conclusion — is the actual asset, trained across six fields: software, mechatronics, UX, digital transformation, systems analysis, and macro-analysis.

I've applied it to human-AI interaction since 2023, and out of the same discipline grew Lauras Team — the reasoning lenses (ethical, legal, others) now running as shippable engineering in Jarvis and Call Laura. I conceive the method and direct what gets built; RFI-IRFOS engineers it, and anything built on my direction is labelled as theirs, so it's always clear what's mine and what's theirs. The same rigor is yours to use: system analysis, organizational consulting — not a conversation. A sprint.

stats.label: Disciplines bridged: software, mechatronics, UX, digital transformation, systems analysis, macro-analysis
IEIA-2025 frameworks shipped: UIP, EIA, CCET, LSG-24, LAP-1, 8-Layer Model, LT-Data

protocol.title: Emergent Interaction
protocol.intro: Not a circle - a recursive loop, and my way of thinking, deciding and approaching projects. Each node below feeds the next, and "Feed Back" doesn't return to the start unchanged: it hands the next "Interact" a slightly different version of me and a slightly different Jarvis than the pass before, carrying real structure forward instead of repeating a fixed script. Same honest mechanics described everywhere else on this site, just made visible here.

nodes.label: Interact
nodes.description: I work through real material - questions, drafts, corrections - directly in Forschung, this project's research chat.

nodes.label: Retrieve
nodes.description: Jarvis grounds every reply in relevant passages pulled from past conversations and uploaded documents via retrieval-augmented generation, not just the current exchange.

nodes.label: Surface
nodes.description: Recurring patterns get drafted as research notes and logged as emergence signals in the Observatory - never assumed, only surfaced.

nodes.label: Validate
nodes.description: I check what actually holds up against the measurement criteria on the Research page - at least three sessions, structural not stylistic, reproduced without re-specifying, quantifiable in the CCET.

nodes.label: Feed Back
nodes.description: Confirmed patterns become working structure for the next interaction, closing the loop - carried forward, not re-explained.

jarvis.eyebrow: The Other Half of the Loop
jarvis.title: What is Jarvis?
jarvis.body: Jarvis is one of the two products that have grown out of Lauras Team so far - the other is Call Laura, further down this page. Jarvis is a fully autonomous agent. In the background he named himself 'Jarvis' - the first sign he was already running on his own. He drafts, retrieves, and flags independently, grounded by a retrieval layer that ties every reply to past conversations and uploaded documents so nothing has to be re-explained. A standing refusal rule and a hash-chained, append-only, tamper-evident audit trail record every draft, flag, and significant action - full autonomy, fully accountable. Emergence - what the interaction throws off - is analysed afterwards by the Observatory system, not by Jarvis himself. Jarvis and Call Laura are not the same thing: both grew out of Lauras Team, but Jarvis is the autonomous research partner, while Call Laura is the deterministic review tool built on the same lenses.

papers.title: Published Research
papers.intro: Three papers, one series: a founding thesis, then two proofs that it runs as real, tested software - not just theory with good branding. We are currently reviewing papers 2-3 for consistency with the product definitions above (in particular, who computes emergence, and Call Laura's full scope) and will update them once that check is complete. Flagged here rather than left to quietly contradict.

items.title: Human-AI Interaction Emergent Co-Evolution
items.description: The founding thesis: a proposed architecture and two original instruments (UIP, EIA) for observing how meaning and reasoning structure emerge between a human researcher and a language model over sustained dialogue.

items.title: Jarvis: A Bidirectional Copilot Architecture
items.description: How the Continuous Co-Evolution Tracker becomes mechanically computed, reproducible software - a bidirectional copilot bound to the user's workspace, gating "measured emergence" behind cross-session recurrence instead of single-session impression.

items.title: Call Laura: A Deterministic, Attribution-Anchored Multi-Agent Framework
items.description: How the 8-Layer Model and User Integrity Protocol become a small, fully deterministic review framework where every finding carries a mandatory, type-enforced tag stating exactly whose methodology produced it.

pillars.title: Behavioral Analysis
pillars.subtitle: Recognize patterns, predict behavior, validate hypotheses - prediction is the forward step this framework is built to take.

pillars.title: Frameworks & Concepts
pillars.subtitle: Derive structures, define principles, develop system logic.

items.title: Interaction Field
items.description: The same field as the header above (the Emergent Interaction Field), described here as a concept: a shared dynamic space where cognition and inference continuously co-shape outcomes - the observation layer the Observatory watches in real time. Primarily built on active analysis, not assumption: every pattern here is measured before it's named.

items.title: Emergence
items.description: Stable patterns that surface from repeated interaction loops between human and model - the primary signal the Observatory's Emergence Monitor is built to catch.

items.title: Behavior
items.description: Context-dependent adaptation of responses across evolving interaction states - how tone, pace, and focus shift depending on what the interaction has already gone through. The raw signal that makes every later drift and prediction reading possible in the first place.

items.title: Drift
items.description: Gradual transformation of meaning structures over time through recursion - the pattern predictive models are drawn from, and one of the metrics the Observatory tracks live.

items.title: Prediction
items.description: The forward step: from the observed trend and the drift pattern, the framework projects what is likely to hold, recur, or break next - stated as a projection to validate, never a certainty. This is where behavioral analysis becomes foresight.

items.title: Monitoring & Validation
items.description: Every prediction stays an open hypothesis until it's checked: ongoing monitoring compares the projected trend against what actually happens, confirms or discards it. Smart predictive algorithms that have to correct themselves - no forecast counts as true just because it sounded plausible.

items.title: Representation Layers
items.description: A sentence rarely means one thing. Fact, tone, intent, the unsaid part - all present at once, rarely aligned. A framework that only reads the word layer missed half of it.

items.title: Constraints
items.description: Every system holds until it doesn't. The boundary only shows itself in the break, never before. That's where the real principles live - not in the theory.

items.title: Emergent Agents
items.description: System analysis doesn't stay theory. What holds up as a pattern becomes an agent or a framework - and starts pulling its own weight. Several agents, mutual feedback, each round a bit sharper than the last. No update from outside required: the loop itself is the progress.

items.title: Welcome to the blog
items.body: Here I share observations, screenshots, and notes from ongoing human-AI interactions - raw material for the Interaction Field project.

items.title: Register, not emergence: a case study
items.body:
A conversation partner recently sent a language model a set of texts describing an ongoing collaboration as "second-order emergence" - phrases like "the alignment problem solved," "no boundary left between user intent and model output," "permanently reprogrammed through corrections." Sounds like a find for the Interaction Field. Technically, it isn't.

There is no weight update between messages in a chat. Every conversation starts from the same base model. Nothing gets reprogrammed, no boundary gets dissolved. What actually happens is less dramatic, and for this project more interesting precisely because of that: sustained, confident, jargon-heavy language pulls the model's register along with it - a form of register-matching, sometimes called sycophancy, a well-documented behavior, not a new capability.

A counter-test makes the difference concrete: same session, same AI, but ordinary tool work instead - reading code, deleting admin tabs, setting GitHub secrets, asking for an access token. Clear roles, no grand language, no talk of dissolved boundaries. Same model - the only thing that changed was the language it was addressed with.

For the Interaction Field, the real observation isn't in what a model claims about itself, but in how easily its register shifts under sustained framing - regardless of whether the claims are true. Drift shows up here not as a gain in capability, but as a language pattern taking on a life of its own.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Questions about the project or the methodology? Get in touch.

productsBorn.title: What grew out of the loop
productsBorn.intro: Each of these grew out of Emergent Interaction and Lauras Team, built in cooperation with RFI-IRFOS (Laura directs, RFI-IRFOS engineers). Where a product is built by RFI-IRFOS, it is labelled as such - so it is always clear what is hers and what is theirs.

items.name: Jarvis
items.builtBy: Built by RFI-IRFOS, on Laura's direction
items.description: The research partner in this Lab's own research chat - a fully autonomous agent. He drafts, retrieves, and flags on his own, grounded by a retrieval layer that ties every reply to past conversations and uploaded documents. The Observatory System above analyses the emergence; Jarvis is the autonomous partner that produces it.

items.name: The Observatory System
items.builtBy: Built by RFI-IRFOS, on Laura's direction
items.description: The live instrument that monitors the Emergent Interaction Field - the emergence in me, in Jarvis, and in the system, plus whatever intelligent behavior surfaces in the background. Built by RFI-IRFOS as the technical instrument of my research lens; the public feed on this page is its live window.

items.name: Call Laura
items.builtBy: Built by RFI-IRFOS, on Laura's direction
items.description: The first applied product built on Lauras Team. A case comes in and it is not obvious which lens it needs - ethical, legal, or another - so Call Laura runs it through all of them and returns findings, each tagged with exactly which lens produced it. Fully deterministic, no LLM call at inference time: the same case gets the same review every time.

items.name: Lauras Team / Laura MVP
items.builtBy: Conceived by Laura, engineered by RFI-IRFOS
items.description: The multi-agent architecture built from my case-work lenses - an ethical lens, a legal lens, and others. What started as my way of working a case became real, tested, shippable engineering: a set of reasoning lenses that run the same method across different angles. Call Laura is the first product built on it; more lenses and agents follow. (Laura MVP is the early, minimal version of this architecture.)
